source.title: Lifting Incident - Deepwater Invictus - Green Canyon Block 868

context.region: Gulf of America - Green Canyon Block 868

actions_taken: IP evacuated by medivac helicopter to University Hospital New Orleans
event.summary: On May 8, 2025, two Transocean engineers were transferring nine sections of Hawse piping to a storage rack on the Deepwater Invictus drillship. After placing the eighth pipe section on the top tier, the 2nd Engineer left to retrieve a U-bolt while the 3rd Engineer began rigging the next pipe. The unsecured pipe section rolled off the rack, fell approximately 3 feet, and crushed the 3rd Engineer's right middle finger, resulting in fingertip amputation at the DIP joint. The incident was caused by failure to secure the pipe before removing lifting equipment, inadequate hazard identification in the pre-job briefing, and flawed storage rack design.
recommendations: BSEE Office of Safety Management to evaluate the Hazard ID Prompt Card (HIPC) used by Transocean
Install improvements to rack design including chalks, bumpers, grooves on supports
Develop written job procedures for pipe transfer tasks
Improve hazard analysis to identify specific pinch points and crush hazards
Ensure pipes are secured before removing lifting equipment
key_phrases: failure to secure pipe before removing chain falls
HIPC did not identify hazard of unsecured pipes rolling

hazards.name: Unsecured pipe on elevated storage rack

threats.name: Pipe not secured before removing lifting equipment
Personnel positioned in drop zone during lifting operations
Inadequate hazard identification in pre-job briefing
Storage rack design allows pipe to roll

controls.name: Hazard ID Prompt Card (HIPC) pre-job briefing
controls.barrier_role: Identify hazards and assign responsibilities before task
controls.lod_basis: First line administrative control to identify hazards before work begins

supporting_text: The HIPC for the task did not identify the need for securing the pipe on the rack before removing the chain falls and did not identify the hazard of unsecured pipes rolling
After reviewing the HIPC, BSEE found it to be inadequate. Although, the HIPC did identify pinch points as a hazard, the HIPC did not specify where the hazard existed
The HIPC failed to recognize unsecured pipe rolling off the rack creating a potential crush point

controls.name: U-bolt securing system for pipes on rack
controls.barrier_role: Physically secure pipes to prevent rolling
controls.lod_basis: Primary physical barrier to prevent pipe movement

supporting_text: After placing the 8th section of pipe on the third (uppermost) tier of the storage rack, the 2E and IP removed the chain falls from the pipe. 2E turned away to retrieve a U-bolt to secure the section of piping
BSEE concluded the largest factor in this incident was the failure to secure the pipe before removing the chain falls
The rack was designed to secure the sections of pipe to the rack with U-bolts

controls.name: Storage rack design with restraints
controls.barrier_role: Passive physical barrier to prevent pipe rolling
controls.lod_basis: Primary engineered control to contain pipes

supporting_text: Equipment failure – flawed design of the storage rack
The design of the rack may not be suitable for pipe support without additional restraints
The TOI report identified improvements like chalks, bumpers, grooves, etc. to be installed on the supports and increasing the angle of the supports

controls.name: Written job procedure for pipe transfer
controls.barrier_role: Provide step-by-step guidance for safe task execution
controls.lod_basis: First line administrative control defining safe work sequence

supporting_text: It was also noted there was no written job procedure for the task being performed
Management system – No written job procedure

controls.name: Safe lifting practices
controls.barrier_role: Ensure proper rigging and lifting techniques

supporting_text: The HIPC also identified safe lifting practices as part of the plan. It did not identify chocking or securing the piping before removing the chain falls
IP placed himself in danger when he knelt on the deck to rig up the next section of Hawse pipe before the previously lifted section of pipe had been secured

controls.name: Personal Protective Equipment (PPE)
controls.barrier_role: Reduce severity of injury to personnel
controls.lod_basis: Last line of defense to protect individual

supporting_text: A positive from the HIPC was that proper PPE was to be worn. The IP's witness statement confirmed that he was wearing the proper PPE

controls.name: Medical evacuation capability
controls.barrier_role: Emergency response to provide medical treatment

supporting_text: The IP was evacuated from the facility by medivac helicopter to University Hospital in New Orleans for treatment

controls.name: Rig hospital and initial medical evaluation

supporting_text: 2E notified the bridge and the IP was escorted to the rig hospital for an initial evaluation

controls.name: Incident notification and investigation procedures
controls.barrier_role: Learn from incident and prevent recurrence

supporting_text: TOI assembled an investigation team and started an investigation into the incident
The Bureau of Safety and Environmental Enforcement (BSEE) Houma District office was notified orally and a written report was submitted in 15 days

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
